page.title: psoriasis vulgaris — Disease Intelligence | OSMF
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for psoriasis vulgaris: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for psoriasis vulgaris: 121 alterations and 242 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Add-on effects of total glucosides of paeony on conventional therapies for psoriasis.
Utilization of light-emitting diodes for skin therapy: Systematic review and meta-analysis.
Analyses suggest modest but significantly elevated levels of the proinflammatory cytokines in the serum of patients with psoriasis.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Spontaneous PASI90 remission essentially zero without treatment; natural fluctuation occurs but complete clearance rare; <5% achieve sustained spontaneous remission over 5 years
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: IL-17/IL-23 biologics achieve PASI100 (complete skin clearance) in 40-87% depending on agent and timeframe; complete clearance is now a realistic treatment goal, not a rarity
remission.gap_size: Large — most patients never reach biologic therapy despite clear eligibility; on-drug clearance rates are exceptional but drug-free remission after cessation is short (12-32 weeks median for PASI loss)
remission.barrier_detail: Most moderate-severe psoriasis patients remain on topicals or older systemics (methotrexate, cyclosporine) for years before biologics are offered; prior authorisation barriers significant; psoriasis is associated with cardiovascular risk requiring systemic treatment beyond skin clearance
remission.last_soc_change: 2020-2022 — IL-23 inhibitors (risankizumab 2019, guselkumab 2017) established complete clearance as the new standard goal; IL-17 inhibitors since 2015; prior biologics (anti-TNF, ustekinumab) since 2009; methotrexate first-line unchanged since 1960s
remission.outdated_treatments: Long-term cyclosporine (nephrotoxicity limits to 1-2 year courses; still used as bridge); PUVA (psoralen + UVA phototherapy — carcinogenic with cumulative exposure; largely replaced by NB-UVB but still prescribed); coal tar preparations (low efficacy, cosmetically problematic, still in OTC products)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Low-dose naltrexone (LDN) — case reports and small series in psoriasis; omega-3 supplementation (reduces PASI in trials); Mediterranean diet (reduces systemic inflammation); topical cannabidiol (early RCT data); stress reduction interventions (psoriasis is stress-responsive)
remission.frontier_signal: Oral IL-23 inhibitors (JNJ-77242113, Phase 2 ANTHEM-PsO); deucravacitinib (TYK2 inhibitor — FDA approved 2022, oral, no black box); bimekizumab (dual IL-17A/F inhibitor — FDA approved 2023); spesolimab (IL-36 receptor inhibitor for GPP — approved 2022)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
